title.zh: We understand the importance of silhouette, fabric structure, and fit balance in modern streetwear hoodie production.
title.en: We understand the importance of silhouette, fabric structure, and fit balance in modern streetwear hoodie production.

content.zh:
In modern streetwear hoodie production, great garments are not created by fabric weight alone. A true heavyweight hoodie is built through the balance of silhouette, fabric structure, and fit proportions.//

Silhouette defines how the hoodie feels visually when worn. Modern streetwear brands focus heavily on shape — including drop shoulders, boxy body proportions, sleeve volume, and overall drape. The goal is not simply to make garments oversized, but to create a strong and intentional silhouette that reflects the identity of the brand.//

Fabric structure plays an equally important role. Two hoodies may use the same GSM weight, but achieve completely different results depending on yarn quality, knitting density, and fabric behavior. A well-structured French Terry fabric provides stability, clean drape, and shape retention, allowing the garment to maintain its intended silhouette after repeated wear and washing.//

Fit balance is what separates premium streetwear hoodies from standard oversized garments. Every proportion must work together — shoulder width, body length, sleeve volume, hood shape, and rib tension. Oversized fit is not about simply scaling up measurements; it requires careful adjustment to preserve comfort, structure, and visual balance.//

At Kellogg, we understand that modern streetwear production is not only about manufacturing clothing. It is about translating a brand’s aesthetic into fabric, silhouette, and fit with consistency and precision.

subtitle.zh: Frequently Asked Questions About Hoodie Manufacturing
subtitle.en: Frequently Asked Questions About Hoodie Manufacturing

question.zh: Q1. What is your MOQ for hoodie production?
question.en: Q1. What is your MOQ for hoodie production?

answer.zh: Answer: Our MOQ for custom hoodie production usually starts from 100–300 pieces per design, depending on fabric, color, and customization requirements.
answer.en: Answer: Our MOQ for custom hoodie production usually starts from 100–300 pieces per design, depending on fabric, color, and customization requirements.

question.zh: Q2. What is the production time for hoodies?
question.en: Q2. What is the production time for hoodies?

answer.zh: Answer: Sample development takes around 7–14 days. Bulk production typically takes 25–35 days after sample approval.
answer.en: Answer: Sample development takes around 7–14 days. Bulk production typically takes 25–35 days after sample approval.

question.zh: Q3. Can you produce heavyweight hoodies (480–600 GSM)?
question.en: Q3. Can you produce heavyweight hoodies (480–600 GSM)?

answer.zh: Answer: Yes, we specialize in heavyweight hoodie production ranging from 480 GSM to 600 GSM, including French terry and brushed fleece fabrics.
answer.en: Answer: Yes, we specialize in heavyweight hoodie production ranging from 480 GSM to 600 GSM, including French terry and brushed fleece fabrics.

question.zh: Q4. Do you offer OEM and ODM services?
question.en: Q4. Do you offer OEM and ODM services?

answer.zh: Answer: Yes, we provide full OEM & ODM services including fabric sourcing, pattern development, sampling, and bulk production.
answer.en: Answer: Yes, we provide full OEM & ODM services including fabric sourcing, pattern development, sampling, and bulk production.

question.zh: Q5. Can you help with fabric and fit development?
question.en: Q5. Can you help with fabric and fit development?

answer.zh: Answer: Yes, we support fabric selection, GSM recommendation, and oversized or drop shoulder fit development for streetwear brands.
answer.en: Answer: Yes, we support fabric selection, GSM recommendation, and oversized or drop shoulder fit development for streetwear brands.

answer.zh: Answer: Yes, we ship globally including the US, Australia, and Europe with export-ready packaging.
answer.en: Answer: Yes, we ship globally including the US, Australia, and Europe with export-ready packaging.
